title: Four Against Darkness - Deep Dive Part 6 - The Mists of Warunor - Part 1
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ssIA_N2u50Y

Daniel (@dungeondive) begins actual gameplay using The Lantern's "Mists of Warunor" campaign, a structured story set in the town of Warner where mysterious fog has begun consuming people and the mayor begs for heroic intervention. Rather than a typical dungeon-focused episode, this video pivots toward demonstrating how to weave together multiple Four Against Darkness supplements into a cohesive campaign narrative. His core question—why isn't there a full supplement dedicated entirely to settlement exploration?—sparks interesting thoughts about games like Legacy of Dragonholt and the untapped potential for town-based FAD expansions.

Daniel walks through Warner's geography: the tavern, blacksmith, elder's cottage, mayor's house, and bulletin board offering side quests. He demonstrates picking a quest (kill 11 goblins, netting a reward), introducing Fiendish Foes as a supplemental monster table for levels 3-5, and showing how The Dreadful Dozen (12 uniquely-detailed bosses) could transform a campaign into a monster-hunting epic. He emphasizes the beauty of Four Against Darkness: its modular nature lets you cherry-pick supplements and house-rules to create your ideal experience without bloating play with excessive complexity.

The episode ends with clear objectives for the next video: venture into the mist-covered plains surrounding Warner, gather six clues, and uncover the truth behind the supernatural fog threatening the village.

----

Would you rather explore a single town repeatedly (discovering its secrets over time) or continuously venture into new dungeons?
